title: test for permissions in bash producing wrong results / faccessat
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

testing for executable in bash appears to be producing wrong resutts

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Run /bin/bash in podman

```
bash-5.1# ls -l /usr/bin/g++-10
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 1258648 Mar 14 12:29 /usr/bin/g++-10
bash-5.1# test -f /usr/bin/g++-10
bash-5.1# echo $?
0
bash-5.1# test -x /usr/bin/g++-10
bash-5.1# echo $?
1
bash-5.1# /usr/bin/g++-10
g++-10: fatal error: no input files
compilation terminated.
bash-5.1# 
```


**Describe the results you received:**

the executable is returning 1 when it should return 0

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.6
Git Commit:   53935fa97ba5d3dfa76fde091d6006655e51e5a3
Built:        Sun Feb 28 04:25:10 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
This seems to be happening with testing for any permissions

```
[user@a5ea681a3714 spack-src]$ test -r src/RngStream.h
[user@a5ea681a3714 spack-src]$ echo $?
1
[user@a5ea681a3714 spack-src]$ test -f src/RngStream.h
[user@a5ea681a3714 spack-src]$ echo $?
0
[user@a5ea681a3714 spack-src]$ ls -l src/R
RngStream.c  RngStream.h  
[user@a5ea681a3714 spack-src]$ ls -l src/RngStream.h
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user 1038 Sep 16  2006 src/RngStream.h
```
It's happening with a particular image.  I tried it on fedora images and it doesn't have this odd behavior.  Any ideas as to what might be causing this?

here is the buildah that generates the image

https://github.com/joequant/bitquant/blob/master/bitstation/01.cauldron/mkimage-buildah-minimal.sh
it seems to happen only with bash, ksh shows correct behavior

It's also definitely a podman issue.  docker with the same image works

```
[joe@big-apple cacher (master)]$ docker run -ti  8c349f5c7006 /bin/bash
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
0
bash-5.1#             
exit
[joe@big-apple cacher (master)]$ podman run -ti  8c349f5c7006 /bin/bash
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
1
bash-5.1# 
exit
```

It has something to do with privileges

```
[joe@big-apple git]$ podman run -ti --privileged 8c349f5c7006   /bin/bash
bash-5.1# cd /var
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
0
bash-5.1# 
exit
[joe@big-apple git]$ podman run -ti  8c349f5c7006   /bin/bash
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
1
bash-5.1# 
```
Any chance you can give a reproducer with an image on a registry I can access?

sure

```
[joe@acer ~]$ podman run -ti docker.io/joequant/cauldron-minimal /bin/bash
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
1
[joe@acer ~]$ podman run -ti --privileged docker.io/joequant/cauldron-minimal /bin/bash
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
0
```
Thanks. Self-assigning, I'll try to get to this early next week.
I vaguely remember running into this problem a long time ago and the issue was something involving has bash deals with capabilities.
Also this is a new problem.  The images seemed to work at the end of last year.  But don't know what changed Mageia or podman.

This worked for me rootless mode.
```
$ rpm -q podman
podman-3.0.1-1.fc34.x86_64
$ sudo podman run -ti docker.io/joequant/cauldron-minimal /bin/bash
[sudo] password for dwalsh: 
Trying to pull docker.io/joequant/cauldron-minimal:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 022040e8eee2 done  
Copying config 8c349f5c70 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
0
```

```
$ podman run -ti docker.io/joequant/cauldron-minimal /bin/bash
Trying to pull docker.io/joequant/cauldron-minimal:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 022040e8eee2 done  
Copying config 8c349f5c70 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
bash-5.1# test -r /var
bash-5.1# echo $?
0
```

Okay, found out something new....

The problem is that bash fails after it runs the command and tries and fails to add an entry into /home/user/.bash_history

There is something odd about my home directory that causes errors

```
[joe@big-apple bitstation (master)]$ podman run -ti joequant/cauldron /bin/bash
[user@d2430bf7ea7c /]$ cd ~user 
[user@d2430bf7ea7c ~]$ cat > test_me
[user@d2430bf7ea7c ~]$ rm test_me
rm: cannot remove 'test_me': Operation not permitted
```

When I look at strace, I'm getting
```
1963920 openat(AT_FDCWD, "/home/user/.bash_history", O_RDONLY) = 3
1963920 fstat(3, {st_mode=S_IFREG|0600, st_size=26, ...}) = 0
1963920 read(3, "ls\ntest -r /media\necho $?\n", 26) = 26
1963920 close(3)                        = 0
1963920 rt_sigprocmask(SIG_BLOCK, [CHLD], [], 8) = 0
1963920 rt_sigprocmask(SIG_SETMASK, [], NULL, 8) = 0
1963920 setpgid(0, 1)                   = -1 EPERM (此項操作並不被允許)
1963920  exit_group(0)                   = ?
```

The command is chinese but it says "command not allowed

could it be an selinux issue?\

Also --cap-add=all doesn't work so what does privilege do that cap-add doesn't

is appears that bash uses faccessat to get permissions while zsh uses access

does that ring any bells?

It appears to be due to this issue that exists in newer glibc.  I'm running 2.33, what you are running

https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1900021

Yeah.  It's a pretty wide spread problem.  Will try with the newest runc

http://patches-tcwg.linaro.org/patch/49774/
Closing this issue as it is addressed in runc and outside of podman
